metadata.name: Trivial World Starter Pack
metadata.description: A sample trivia pack covering all six categories: geography, pop culture, history, art, science, and sports.
metadata.author: Trivial World Team

questions.questionText: In which country is the city of Istanbul located?
questions.tidbits: Canada has the longest coastline of any country in the world, stretching over 202,000 km.

questions.questionText: What is the tallest mountain in Africa?
questions.tidbits: The Sahara Desert spans 11 countries and is roughly the size of the contiguous United States.

questions.questionText: In Neon Genesis Evangelion, what is the name of the futuristic city built over the Geofront?
questions.tidbits: The Amazon River discharges about 20% of all fresh water that flows into the world's oceans.

questions.questionText: What is the capital city of Brazil?
questions.tidbits: The Great Wall of China stretches over 13,000 miles when including all its branches and sections.

questions.questionText: In which US state is the Grand Canyon located?
questions.tidbits: Antarctica is technically a desert — it receives very little precipitation annually.

questions.questionText: What sea lies between Italy and Croatia?

What is the largest desert in the world by area?
In Attack on Titan, what is the name of the outermost wall protecting humanity?
What is the only country in the world that is also a continent?

questions.tidbits: The first video game to feature Mario was Donkey Kong (1981), where he was called "Jumpman."

questions.questionText: On which platform did PewDiePie become the most subscribed individual creator?
questions.tidbits: Netflix was founded in 1997 as a DVD mail-rental service before pivoting to streaming in 2007.

questions.questionText: Who plays Tony Stark / Iron Man in the Marvel Cinematic Universe?
questions.tidbits: The Marvel Cinematic Universe is the highest-grossing film franchise in history, surpassing $30 billion in box office revenue.

questions.questionText: On which streaming platform can you watch Stranger Things?
questions.tidbits: YouTube was founded in February 2005 and acquired by Google just 18 months later for $1.65 billion.

questions.questionText: What is the name of the popular internet meme featuring a dog sitting calmly in a burning room?
questions.tidbits: Minecraft has sold over 238 million copies across all platforms, making it the best-selling video game of all time.

questions.questionText: What is the real name of the streamer known as Ninja?
In the MCU, what Infinity Stone was hidden on the planet Vormir?
Which YouTuber is famous for the series "It's Everyday Bro" and became one of the most subscribed channels?

In what year was the first iPhone released by Apple? A) 2005, B) 2006, C) 2007, D) 2008
questions.tidbits: The Great Pyramids of Giza were built with such precision that the sides of the Great Pyramid are aligned almost exactly to the cardinal directions.

questions.questionText: Who is widely credited as the world's first computer programmer?
questions.tidbits: The Roman Empire, at its peak, connected approximately 20% of the world's population under one government.

questions.questionText: What ancient Greek city-state was renowned for its warrior culture and military training?
questions.tidbits: The Black Death in the 14th century killed an estimated one-third to one-half of Europe's population.

questions.questionText: In which battle did Napoleon Bonaparte suffer his final defeat in 1815?
questions.tidbits: The Wright Brothers' first powered flight at Kitty Hawk lasted only 12 seconds and covered 120 feet.

questions.questionText: Who invented the World Wide Web in 1989?
questions.tidbits: Neil Armstrong's famous quote was likely a miscommunication — he intended to say "one small step for a man."

questions.questionText: What Japanese warrior class followed the code of Bushido?
In what year did the first crewed Moon landing occur? A) 1965, B) 1967, C) 1969, D) 1972
Which computer scientist coined the term "bug" for a computing error after finding an actual insect in a computer?
What ancient wonder of the world stood at the entrance to the harbor of Rhodes?
In which year did the Berlin Wall fall? A) 1987, B) 1988, C) 1990, D) 1989
Who created Batman — originally appearing in Detective Comics #27 in 1939?
questions.tidbits: The Mona Lisa is painted on a poplar wood panel, not canvas, and is smaller than most people expect — about 30 by 21 inches.

questions.questionText: What is Spider-Man's real name?
questions.tidbits: Shakespeare invented over 1,700 words that we still use today, including "bedroom," "lonely," and "generous."

questions.questionText: Who painted the Mona Lisa?
questions.tidbits: The first comic book superhero is generally considered to be Superman, who debuted in Action Comics #1 in 1938.

questions.questionText: In which country did manga (Japanese comics) originate?
questions.tidbits: The Louvre in Paris is the world's most visited art museum, welcoming about 9 million visitors per year.

questions.questionText: What art movement, characterized by bold imagery from popular culture, includes artists like Andy Warhol and Roy Lichtenstein?
Who wrote the graphic novel Watchmen, published by DC Comics in 1986?
Which superhero is known as the Man of Steel and comes from the planet Krypton?
Who created the X-Men for Marvel Comics?
What animation studio produced the films Spirited Away and My Neighbor Totoro?
What is the name of the underground city where the Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles originally lived?
questions.answerText: The sewers of New York City

questions.questionText: What does the abbreviation "AI" stand for?
questions.tidbits: A teaspoon of neutron star material would weigh about 10 million tons — roughly the weight of Mount Everest.

questions.questionText: How many planets are currently recognized in our solar system? A) 7, B) 8, C) 9, D) 10
questions.tidbits: DNA was first identified in 1869, but its double-helix structure wasn't discovered until Watson and Crick's work in 1953.

questions.questionText: What animal is considered the apex predator of the ocean?
questions.tidbits: The Internet was originally developed as ARPANET in 1969, funded by the U.S. Department of Defense.

questions.questionText: What company created and released ChatGPT?
questions.tidbits: Octopuses have three hearts, blue blood, and can change color despite being colorblind.

questions.questionText: What is the name of NASA's Mars rover that landed in Jezero Crater in February 2021?
questions.tidbits: The human brain generates about 20 watts of power — enough to power a dim light bulb.

questions.questionText: What boundary surrounds a black hole beyond which nothing — not even light — can escape?
What is the fastest land animal in the world?
Who proposed the "Turing Test" as a way to measure machine intelligence?
What type of star is our Sun classified as on the Hertzsprung-Russell diagram?
What is the name of the neural network architecture that introduced the "attention" mechanism and became the foundation of modern LLMs?
Which esports game features the annual championship tournament called "The International"?
questions.tidbits: Michael Jordan was cut from his high school varsity basketball team as a sophomore.

questions.questionText: How many players from each team are on the court at the same time in basketball? A) 4, B) 5, C) 6, D) 7
questions.tidbits: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens, Greece in 1896 with 241 athletes from 14 nations.

questions.questionText: Which college football team is nicknamed the Fighting Irish?
questions.tidbits: Babe Ruth started his career as a pitcher for the Boston Red Sox before becoming a legendary home run hitter for the Yankees.

questions.questionText: In which competitive first-person shooter game is the ESL Pro League a major tournament?
questions.tidbits: Soccer (association football) is the most-watched sport in the world, with an estimated 3.5 billion fans globally.

questions.questionText: How many holes make up a standard round of golf? A) 9, B) 12, C) 18, D) 24
questions.tidbits: The Super Bowl is consistently the most-watched annual television broadcast in the United States.

questions.questionText: Which country won the first-ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
What is the name of the annual championship game of the NFL?
In esports, what does the term "clutch" typically mean?
questions.answerText: Winning a round or match despite being outnumbered or in a disadvantaged position
